evals.prompt: My React app fetches user data, then their orders, then each order's products in sequence. It's very slow.
evals.expected_output: Sequential awaits cause request waterfalls. Fix: parallelize with Promise.all([getUser(id), getOrders(id), getProducts(ids)]). Use Suspense boundaries to stream results as they arrive. If using React Router, initiate fetches in route loaders before rendering.

evals.prompt: Our bundle size is 4MB and the initial page load is very slow.
evals.expected_output: Reduce bundle size: (1) No barrel files — import directly from component files. (2) Use React.lazy/dynamic for heavy components (charts, modals). (3) Replace moment with dayjs, lodash with radash. (4) Analyze with source-map-explorer to find bloat. (5) Enable tree-shaking by using named exports only (no export *).

evals.prompt: My Context causes all consumers to re-render every time any value changes. There are 50+ components subscribed.
evals.expected_output: Split Context into State and Dispatch objects. Consumers that only read dispatch (setters) won't re-render when state changes. For high-frequency updates across many components: switch to Zustand or Jotai. Use react-window for list virtualization if rendering many items. Extract static JSX outside component scope.

should_not_trigger: Write a Python script for data processing
Set up a Go gRPC server
